summary:
I have found the issue and fixed the certificate chain ordering in `app/tlssetup/loader.go`.

The `tls.X509KeyPair` function parses the PEM certificates in the exact order they appear in the file. If the original `.pem` file presents them out of order (e.g., leaf, root, intermediate), strict clients that expect RFC 5246 compliant chains will reject the connection.

I updated `LoadTLSConfig` to dynamically sort the loaded certificates so that each subsequent certificate correctly validates as the issuer of the previous one (i.e., `cert[i].Issuer == cert[i+1].Subject`).

The verification test `test.sh` now passes successfully.
